calendar.matters:
SMALL CLAIM APPEALS
Off Calendar Aug-14-2013 Judgment Entered
MINI MINUTES:
Judge Pro Tem Elliot Myles; Clerk: B.Paredes; not reported; (525).

Cause called for small Claims appeal. Appearance by Darius T. Chan, counsel for Respondent/Plaintiff Hon Lam Yueng; Anthony Tam Cantonese interpreter for plaintiff is sworn.
Appearance by Fayedine Coulter, counsel for Appellant/Defendant Clifford Jung.
Plaintiff Yueng and Paul Mochizuki on behalf of defendant are sworn and examined.

1-Attorney Coulter did not file a substitution of attorney for appellant Cliffor Jung, and has no standing to represent him in court.

2-Paul Mochizuki is a proper representative for appellant Clifford Jung under CCP 116.540, and is represented by his attorney Fayedine Coulter.

3-Appellant Clifford Jung orally stipulated to Judge Pro Tem Elliot Myles on May 2, 2013 and June 6, 2013 and did not withdraw his stipulation.

4- Motion to continue under 8.811(b) made by Paul Mochizuki on behalf of appellant is DENIED.

5- Parties are to file post hearing briefs of no more than five pages on or before August 9, 2013, with a courtesy copy delivered by E-mail to the Judge Pro Tem.

SMALL CLAIM APPEALS
Off Calendar Jun-06-2013 Continued To Aug-01-13 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 525
MINI MINUTES: 9:05am
Judge Pro Tem Elliot Myles. Clerk: M. Goodman. Not reported. (525)

Cause called for Small Claims Appeal. Appearance by Andrew H. Dai, Counsel for Respondent/Plaintiff Hon Lam Yeung. No Apperance by Defendant/Appellant. Counsel previously sworn.

Counsel requests continuance to August 1, 2013.

On Court's Own Motion, matter is continued to August 01, 2013 at 9:00 am D525.

SMALL CLAIM APPEALS
Off Calendar May-02-2013 Continued To Jun-06-13 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 525
MINI MINUTES: 9:10am
Judge Pro Tem Elliott Myles, clerk J. Kong, not reported (D525)
Cause called for Small Claims Appeal. Appellant, no appearance; respondent, Hon Lam Yeung appears with attoney Andrew Dai. Party, previusly sworn and respondent requests a continuance because this case may consolidate with the civil case (CGC-12-526785).

Matter is continued to June 6, 2013.
